name: IMP4 — conditional conformal vs the absolute high-synergy coverage bar
status: PRE-REGISTERED BEFORE RUNNING. Frozen so a failure is an honest negative.
motivation: The SCC scalar-normalizer reduces the miscoverage SLOPE but does not clear the ABSOLUTE high-synergy coverage bar (top-50% high-synergy coverage CI includes 0.90 from below on Norman; not cleared on CaRPool). Question: does an explicitly GROUP-CONDITIONAL conformal method (Mondrian by synergy tercile) or a covariate-conditional method (Gibbs-Cherian-Candes) clear the ABSOLUTE bar without redefining it?
methods_compared: conditional_quantile_shat (regress nonconformity quantile on synergy prior)
primary_endpoint: high-synergy (top-50%) empirical coverage with 5000x bootstrap 95% CI; a method CLEARS iff its high-synergy coverage CI CONTAINS 0.90 AND its point coverage >= 0.88, on BOTH datasets, at nominal 0.90.
secondary: mean interval width (efficiency) and marginal coverage; a method that clears by inflating width to infinity is noted as trivial.
no_phacking_guards: the 0.90 nominal and top-50% split are fixed from the SCC prereg, not re-chosen
width reported alongside coverage so trivial-inflation is visible
all methods use identical LOPO folds and identical nonconformity base score |y-additive|
honest_prior: 40% that any conditional method cleanly clears the absolute bar on BOTH datasets; conditional methods typically restore coverage at a width cost.
